hero.subtitle: We open Uzbekistan's digital economy to the world — guiding international companies and investors from first meeting to fully operational.

why.label: Why Uzbekistan
why.title: The opportunity is here. So is the timing.
paragraphs: Uzbekistan's digital economy is already in motion. IT service exports crossed $1 billion in 2025. Nearly 3,000 companies operate inside the national IT Park. The Digital Uzbekistan 2030 strategy is a national priority, backed at the highest level of government with the institutions and incentives required to deliver it.

For technology companies, the fiscal environment is exceptionally competitive. IT Park residents are exempt from corporate tax, VAT, and social tax. Personal income is subject to a flat rate of 7.5 percent. Market entry is structured to be fast and fully supported, with streamlined registration and a dedicated team guiding every step.

The talent pipeline is substantial. A population of 37 million, majority under thirty, produces tens of thousands of IT graduates annually. The workforce operates across Uzbek, Russian and English, and a national program is currently training one million new specialists in digital skills.
Investors who establish early in emerging digital markets define the conditions for those who follow. Uzbekistan is at that inflection point.

hero.title: The gateway to Uzbekistan's digital economy.
hero.subtitle: Established in 2023 as a structural unit of the Ministry of Digital Technologies, the Investment Project Office advances the nation's digital transformation in line with the Digital Uzbekistan 2030 Strategy — and helps the world take part in it.

mission.title: Driving digital growth through strategic IT investment.
body: The Investment Project Office is a structural unit under the Ministry of Digital Technologies of the Republic of Uzbekistan. Its main task is to attract new investments, grants and sponsorship funds in the field of digital technologies, and to support foreign investors in entering and expanding within Uzbekistan's IT sector.

The Office provides comprehensive market-entry assistance and full business support — including company registration, legal and administrative guidance, facilitation of residence and tax procedures, and connections with local service providers.

programs.title: Purpose-built to bring you in.
programs.intro: Uzbekistan has built a suite of programs specifically to lower the barrier for foreign technology companies entering the market.

items.name: IT Park Zero Risk
items.desc: Residents pay no corporate, VAT or social tax, and a flat 7.5% personal income tax. Companies can be fully registered and operational within weeks of arrival.

items.name: IT-Visa
items.desc: Streamlined residence for IT founders, investors and specialists, valid for up to three years.

items.name: Soft-Landing
items.desc: Practical, hands-on help to get your team on the ground and operational quickly.

items.name: Digital Startups
items.desc: Support pathways and funding routes for scalable digital startups and IT Park residents.

hero.title: Investment projects in Uzbekistan's digital sector.
hero.subtitle: From landmark AI infrastructure to export-oriented delivery and creative industries — a portfolio designed to strengthen the national technology landscape and deliver returns.

portfolio.label: Investment portfolio
portfolio.title: The deals shaping Uzbekistan's digital decade.
portfolio.intro: A record of the investments, partnerships and national programs delivered with the Ministry of Digital Technologies. Select a year to explore, and open any project to read the detail.
portfolio.hint: Select a project to read more

deals.title: Shanghai Linkwise Modular Computing Centre
deals.desc: A $3.5 billion foreign direct investment to build a Modular Intelligent Computing Centre in the Republic of Karakalpakstan, planned for commissioning in 2027 — among the largest technology FDI commitments in the country's history.

deals.title: DataVolt Green Data Centres
deals.desc: Saudi Arabia's DataVolt is developing green data centres in Uzbekistan under a foreign-investment program worth up to $5 billion by 2030. Its first facility, the 12 MW TAS-1 in Tashkent, is fully foreign-financed with $150 million backing from DEG, the EBRD, the OPEC Fund and Proparco.

deals.title: Five Million AI Leaders
deals.desc: A national program launched with the United Arab Emirates to train five million citizens in artificial intelligence by 2030 — 4.75 million students, 150,000 teachers and 100,000 civil servants. More than one million people have already completed basic training.

deals.title: Nvidia Supercomputer Cluster
deals.desc: A major national supercomputer cluster being launched in partnership with Nvidia to power domestic AI development, alongside a National Transfer Office opening in Silicon Valley to connect Uzbek founders with global investors.

deals.title: $1 Billion in IT Service Exports
deals.desc: IT service exports crossed $1 billion for the first time in the country's history, on the path to a national target of $5 billion in annual digital exports by 2030.

deals.title: Japan Animation Talent Initiative
deals.desc: A partnership with Japan's ELF-IN, backed by a grant exceeding $700,000, to train a new generation of animation and computer-graphics professionals and build an export-ready creative industry.

deals.title: ICT Week Investment Forum
deals.desc: Tashkent hosted the IT Investment Forum during ICT Week 2025, convening government leaders, global companies and international investors around Uzbekistan's digital opportunity.

deals.title: Record FDI Inflows
deals.desc: Foreign direct investment reached $11.9 billion, up more than 50% year on year and equal to 10.3% of GDP — a clear signal of rising international confidence in Uzbekistan.

deals.title: DataVolt Green Data Centre Agreement
deals.desc: Uzbekistan signed with Saudi Arabia's DataVolt to develop a network of green data centres, laying the groundwork for a multi-billion-dollar build-out of the country's digital infrastructure.

hero.title: Where the Silk Road meets the digital age.
hero.subtitle: For centuries, Uzbekistan connected the world's markets. Today it does so in code. Tell us what you're planning, and we'll map the path to market.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
body: This is a placeholder privacy policy for the Investment Project Office website. Final legal text will be provided before launch.

We collect only the information you choose to share with us — such as your name, email and message when you contact the office. We use it solely to respond to your enquiry and to support your engagement with Uzbekistan's digital sector.
We do not sell your data. Aggregated, anonymised analytics may be used to improve the website. You may request access to, or deletion of, your data by contacting invest@digital.uz.
